name: George Benson
abstract: George Washington Benson (born March 22, 1943) is an American jazz fusion guitarist, singer, and songwriter. He began his professional career at the age of 19 as a jazz guitarist.

interviews.title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: He played on my first record... That’s how I met George. Then I ended up playing on live concerts with him at Carnegie Hall... on that tour, George Benson, Bob James, Ron Carter...
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Easley
about_quotes.evidence: By January of ’68 I’m out on the road with George Benson. You understand? For two years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Easley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 1, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: I spent many, many years as you know, with Horace Silver and George Benson... and I got a call one day from Charles Tolliver during my stint with George Benson in 1971
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: And George, the next record he made with me, which was a hit, and then he went on to CTI, started singing, and that was a hit.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: even George Benson used to come down there and play with me. He's a good friend....

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: when I worked and made the first record for McDuff, him and George Benson and Joe Dukes had been playing together... And I joined his band... So when we went to the session that night, it was perfect. The people... Whereas George and McDuff was playing the harmony.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995
